title: OREGON MEDICAL MARIJUANA PROGRAM - 211info
text: OREGON HEALTH AUTHORITY PUBLIC HEALTH DIVISION OREGON MEDICAL MARIJUANA PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Processes applications of Oregonians suffering from qualifying debilitating medical conditions for the use of medicinal marijuana without the fear of civil or criminal penalties when their doctors advise that such use may provide a medical benefit. 800 NE Oregon Street Portland, OR 97232 Eligibility: Varies Hours: Monday-Friday noon-4pm Email (971) 673-1234 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers (971) 673-1222 Main phone (971) 673-1299 FAX 711 TTY/TTD (800) 422-6012 Toll-free Main phone (971) 673-1234 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call for information or see website. See application for eligibility requirements. FEES: $200 for new or renewal application $60 for SNAP (food stamps) recipients $50 for those currently enrolled in OHP $20 for persons receiving Supplemental Security Income (SSI) $20 for veterans of the US Armed Forces DOCUMENTS: None required at intake AREA SERVED: State of Oregon Services: Medical Marijuana Registration Programs Other Services Offered At This Location: COMMUNITY GROUPS AND GOVERNMENT/ADMINISTRATIVE OFFICES - ALERT IMMUNIZATION INFORMATION SYSTEM COMMUNITY GROUPS AND GOVERNMENT/ADMINISTRATIVE OFFICES - OREGON SCREENWISE PROGRAM CONSUMER REGULATION - BIRTH CERTIFICATES CONSUMER REGULATION - OREGON VITAL RECORDS CONSUMER REGULATION - EARLY HEARING DETECTION AND INTERVENTION SPECIALIZED TREATMENT AND PREVENTION - VACCINES FOR CHILDREN SUBSTANCE USE DISORDER SERVICES - OREGON HOPELINE SUBSTANCE USE DISORDER SERVICES - TOBACCO QUIT LINE If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service.
